success_surface: Successfully extracted numeric values from complex currency-formatted strings and wrote results to a targeted cell range without affecting unmodified rows.
effective_behaviors: Restricted modifications to the exact answer_position range without modifying adjacent or unused cells
Used openpyxl for both reading and writing to preserve original workbook structure
Implemented defensive parsing logic with null checks and type validation before writing values
Iterated over all source rows but conditionally wrote only to specified target rows
Performed post-write verification by reloading output file to confirm changes
evidence: Conditional write: if 2 <= excel_row <= 5 ensures only target cells are modified
openpyxl load_workbook() used for input reading preserving non-formula cell values
parse_currency_string() returns None for invalid inputs, preventing bad data propagation
data_rows collected then written conditionally based on row number matching answer_position
Re-opened saved file with load_workbook() to print verification output for critical cells

memory_items.title: Strict Range Adherence
memory_items.lesson: When instructions specify a target cell range, modify only those cells even when processing broader source data
memory_items.generalization: Always filter write operations to match the specified answer_position before modifying any cells

memory_items.title: Read-Write Tool Consistency
memory_items.lesson: Use openpyxl for both reading and writing workbooks to maintain cell type integrity across the entire operation
memory_items.generalization: Maintain tool consistency throughout read-modify-write cycles to avoid format conversion issues

memory_items.title: Defensive Value Parsing
memory_items.lesson: Validate and sanitize data during parsing to handle nulls, malformed values, and edge cases before writing
memory_items.generalization: Implement graceful failure modes in string-to-value conversion functions to prevent corrupt outputs

memory_items.title: Post-Modification Verification
memory_items.lesson: Re-open the saved output file and inspect critical cells to confirm correct value placement
memory_items.generalization: Include verification steps after writing to ensure target cells contain expected values

edits.content:
## Additional Best Practices

### Strict Range Adherence
- When a target cell range is specified (e.g., via answer_position), filter all write operations to affect only those cells.
- Do not overwrite adjacent rows/columns unless explicitly instructed to do so.
- Collect all processing results first, then write selectively to confirmed target coordinates.

### Read-Write Tool Consistency
- Use openpyxl for both reading input workbooks and writing output files to maintain consistent behavior.
- Avoid mixing pandas and openpyxl in the same read-modify-write cycle unless necessary for calculations.
- Be aware that different tools may handle cell types differently (numbers vs strings vs formulas).

### Defensive Value Parsing
- Implement null/empty checks before attempting string-to-value conversions.
- Handle multiple format variants (currency symbols, thousand separators, decimal commas) robustly.
- Return safe defaults (None/blank) when parsing fails rather than raising exceptions that halt execution.

### Post-Modification Verification
- After saving the workbook, reload it using a fresh Workbook object to verify critical cells.
- Print or log verified values for target positions to confirm successful updates.
- Note: openpyxl Workbook objects do not support context managers—use manual close() or structured exception handling.
